name: American Football
description: A contact sport played on a rectangular field where teams advance an oval ball by running or passing to score touchdowns. Dominant in the United States, governed domestically by the NFL.

name: Archery
description: The sport of shooting arrows at a target using a bow. Includes recurve, compound, and barebow disciplines. Has been an Olympic sport since the 1900 Games, reintroduced permanently in 1972.

name: Athletics (Track and Field)
description: The oldest organized sport, encompassing running, jumping, throwing, and combined events. A cornerstone of the Olympic programme since the first modern Games in 1896.

name: Badminton
description: A racquet sport played with a shuttlecock across a net, in singles or doubles format. Particularly popular in Asia. Has been an Olympic sport since 1992.

name: Baseball
description: A bat-and-ball sport where a pitcher throws to a batter who attempts to hit the ball and run bases to score runs. A defining sport of American culture, governed by Major League Baseball at the top level.

name: Basketball
description: A team sport where players score by shooting a ball through the opposing team's hoop. Invented by James Naismith in 1891 and now one of the most popular sports globally. Has been in the Olympics since 1936.

name: Boxing
description: A combat sport in which two competitors fight with their fists, wearing padded gloves, within a roped ring. One of the oldest sports, it has been part of the Olympics since the 1904 St. Louis Games.

name: Chess
description: A two-player strategy board game played on an 8×8 grid. Recognized by the International Olympic Committee (IOC) as a sport since 1999. One of the most played and studied games in history, with formal world championship competitions since 1886.

name: Cricket
description: A bat-and-ball team sport played on a circular field with a rectangular 22-yard pitch at its centre. Extremely popular in South Asia, the UK, Australia, and the Caribbean. Formats range from 5-day Test matches to 20-over T20 games.

name: Cycling
description: Competition using bicycles across road, track, mountain, and BMX disciplines. Has been part of every Olympic Games since 1896. Major events include the Tour de France, Giro d'Italia, and Vuelta a España.

name: Equestrian
description: Olympic equestrian sports include dressage, show jumping, and eventing. Unique in being one of the few Olympic sports where men and women compete together on equal terms and an animal is integral to the competition.

name: Fencing
description: A combat sport using one of three weapons: foil, épée, or sabre. One of only five sports to have been featured at every modern Olympic Games since 1896. Scoring is electronic in modern competition.

name: Figure Skating
description: A winter sport in which individuals or pairs perform spins, jumps, and footwork on ice. Among the most popular events at the Winter Olympics, with disciplines including singles, pairs, ice dance, and team events.

name: Football (Soccer)
description: The most popular sport globally, played with a round ball on a rectangular pitch. The objective is to score by getting the ball into the opposing team's goal. Governed by FIFA, with the FIFA World Cup as the most-watched sporting event in the world.

name: Formula 1
description: The highest class of single-seater open-wheel auto racing, sanctioned by the FIA. The Formula One World Championship has been held since 1950. Teams invest heavily in aerodynamics and engine technology to compete across a global calendar.

name: Golf
governing_body: Royal and Ancient Golf Club (R&A) / USGA
description: A club-and-ball sport in which players use various clubs to hit balls into a series of holes on a course in as few strokes as possible. Returned to the Olympics in 2016 after a 112-year absence. Major tournaments include the Masters, The Open, US Open, and PGA Championship.

name: Gymnastics
description: A sport involving the performance of exercises requiring balance, strength, flexibility, agility, and coordination. Olympic disciplines include artistic, rhythmic, and trampolining. Has been part of every modern Olympic Games.

name: Handball
description: A fast-paced indoor team sport where players throw a ball with their hands to score into the opposing goal. Particularly popular in Europe. Has been a permanent Olympic sport since 1972 for men and 1976 for women.

name: Hockey (Field)
description: A team sport played on grass or turf where players use hooked sticks to drive a ball into the opposing goal. An Olympic sport for men since 1908 and women since 1980. Particularly popular in the Netherlands, India, and Australia.

name: Hockey (Ice)
description: A team sport played on an ice rink in which players use sticks to shoot a rubber puck into the opponent's net. Canada's national winter sport. The NHL is the top professional league globally. Has been in the Winter Olympics since 1920.

name: Judo
description: A martial art and combat sport originating in Japan that focuses on throwing and grappling techniques. Created by Jigoro Kano in 1882. Has been an Olympic sport for men since 1964 and for women since 1992.

name: Karate
description: A striking martial art using punches, kicks, knee strikes, and open-hand techniques. Featured at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics but not included in future editions. Practiced worldwide both as a sport and for self-defence.

name: Lacrosse
description: A team sport using a long-handled stick with a mesh net (crosse) to carry, pass, and shoot a small rubber ball into the opposing goal. One of the oldest team sports in North America, originating with Indigenous peoples. Returning to the Olympics in 2028.

name: MotoGP
description: The premier class of motorcycle road racing, sanctioned by the FIM. The MotoGP World Championship has been held since 1949. Races take place on purpose-built circuits worldwide with prototype 1000cc motorcycles.

name: Rowing
description: A water sport in which athletes race in boats using oars. Includes single sculls through to eight-person sweep events. Has been part of the Olympics since 1900. Famous events include the Oxford-Cambridge Boat Race.

name: Rugby League
description: A form of rugby played with 13 players per side, with a play-the-ball rule after tackles. Popular in England, Australia, New Zealand, and Papua New Guinea. Differs from rugby union in scoring, rules, and professional structure.

name: Rugby Union
description: A contact team sport in which players carry, pass, and kick an oval ball to score tries or goals. The Rugby World Cup is one of the most-watched sporting events globally. The 7-a-side variant (Sevens) has been an Olympic sport since 2016.

name: Sailing
description: A water sport using the force of wind to propel boats across a course. Has been part of the Olympics since 1900. Classes range from single-handed dinghies to keelboats. Famous events include the America's Cup and Vendée Globe.

name: Skiing and Snowboarding
description: Snow sports including alpine skiing, cross-country skiing, ski jumping, biathlon, and snowboarding. Alpine skiing and cross-country have been in the Winter Olympics since 1936; snowboarding since 1998. Norway and Alpine nations dominate traditional disciplines.

name: Snooker
governing_body: World Professional Billiards and Snooker Association (WPBSA)
description: A cue sport played on a large cloth-covered table, in which players use a cue to pot coloured balls into pockets in a specific order. The World Snooker Championship, held at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, is the sport's most prestigious event.

name: Speed Skating
description: A competitive form of ice skating in which competitors race each other over a set distance on an oval ice track. Long track and short track are both Olympic disciplines. The Netherlands has historically dominated long track events.

name: Squash
description: A racquet sport played by two players in a four-walled court with a small rubber ball. Known for its intense cardiovascular demands. Not yet an Olympic sport, though repeatedly proposed for inclusion. Egypt currently dominates world rankings.

name: Sumo
description: A form of competitive full-contact wrestling where a rikishi (wrestler) attempts to force an opponent out of a circular ring (dohyo) or to touch the ground with any body part other than the soles of the feet. A Japanese national sport with centuries of tradition.

name: Swimming
description: An individual or team racing sport requiring the use of one's entire body to move through water using one of four strokes: freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke, and butterfly. Has been part of the Olympics since 1896. Michael Phelps holds the record for most Olympic medals.

name: Table Tennis
description: A racquet sport played on a table divided by a net. One of the fastest racquet sports in the world. China has dominated international table tennis for decades. Has been an Olympic sport since 1988.

name: Taekwondo
description: A Korean martial art and combat sport characterized by its emphasis on head-height kicks, jumping and spinning kicks, and fast kicking techniques. Became a full Olympic sport in 2000 at the Sydney Games.

name: Tennis
description: A racquet sport played on a rectangular court with a net, in singles or doubles format. The four Grand Slam tournaments—Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open—are the sport's most prestigious events. Has been part of the Olympics since 1988.

name: Triathlon
description: A multi-discipline endurance sport combining consecutive swimming, cycling, and running events. The Olympic distance is 1.5 km swim, 40 km bike, 10 km run. Became an Olympic sport in 2000. The Ironman distance (3.8/180/42.2 km) is the sport's ultimate challenge.

name: Volleyball
description: A team sport in which six players on each side use their hands or arms to volley a ball over a high net. Both indoor volleyball and beach volleyball (two-player) are Olympic sports. Brazil, the USA, and Russia have historically dominated.

name: Water Polo
description: A competitive team water sport played in a swimming pool where teams attempt to throw a ball into the opposing team's goal. Has been an Olympic sport for men since 1900 and for women since 2000. Hungary has historically been the most successful nation.

name: Wrestling
description: One of the oldest sports in the world, involving grappling techniques to pin or gain points over an opponent. Olympic disciplines include freestyle and Greco-Roman for men, and freestyle for women. Has been part of the Olympics since the ancient Games.
